auth.welcomeTitle: Welcome back to your business platform
auth.welcomeSub: Sign in to manage sales, inventory, accounting and your team.
auth.brandFooter: © 2026 iDoc ERP · Secured with multi-tenant + RLS
auth.title: Sign in
auth.subtitle: Sign in with your email — we'll take you to your company.
auth.devHint: System owner (dev): {{email}} / {{password}} — or use the Gmail button. Production enforces Google.

auth.gmail_not_enabled: This email is not enabled for Gmail sign-in

company.not_found: No company found for this user

home.reportsDesc: Business overview, analytics and deep insights
home.adminDesc: Settings, users, permissions and platform administration

companies.createdNoEmail: Starts on Trial (no email given, so no login account)

err.requireCodeName: Please enter company code and name

drawer.title: New company
drawer.name: Company name
drawer.note: New company starts on "Trial". If an email is given, an owner account is created with default password {{password}} (changeable later).

hr.title: People (HR)
hr.notLoggedIn: Not signed in to any company — please sign in first

emp.empty: No employees in this company yet — click "Add employee"
emp.clickToView: Click a row to view details

position.hint: Define which modules this position can access
position.name: Position name

empForm.noPermYet: This position has no access set
empForm.pickToSeePerm: Select a position to see its bound access
empForm.permHint: Access is bound to the position — edit at People › Positions
empForm.addressFullPh: Type sub-district / district / province / postal code
empForm.passwordEditPh: set/change password — at least 4 chars (blank = keep current)
empForm.gmailToggle: Allow this employee to sign in with Gmail (via the email above)
empForm.notePassword: Set a password = sign in with email/password ·
empForm.noteGmail: Enable Gmail = sign in via Google with this email (email required in Contact)
empForm.emptyHint: No data entered in this section

hrSettings.title: HR settings
hrSettings.subtitle: Module-level settings for People (HR)
hrSettings.enforceOffHint: Off: anyone can open Settings (prevents locking yourself out).
hrSettings.enforceOnHint: On: only an HR super admin can open Settings.
hrSettings.willLoseAccess: Warning: you are not an HR super admin — turning this on will lock you out of Settings.
hrSettings.noAccess: Permission enforcement is on — only an HR super admin can access Settings.

empFields.title: Employee fields
empFields.hint: Choose which fields your company collects. Core fields are always on.
empFields.dragHint: Drag fields between columns, or use + / ×
empFields.optionsHint: Configure choices for dropdown fields — add or remove

posForm.name: Position name *
posForm.modulesHint: Modules this position can access (employees inherit it)

empDetail.soonSub: Menus for this employee will be available shortly

customer.title: Customers
customer.hintSoon: In development — you can start adding customers soon
customer.clickToView: Click a row to view details
customer.groupHint: Click a group to view its customers

move.head: 200 most recently active
move.hint: acted on by a tool, a document (CL/FO/QT/SO) or the calendar

readyFilter.label: Count only
readyFilter.needConfig: — enable a readiness rule in settings first

custSearch.title: Search settings (full)
custSearch.dragHint: Drag to reorder · choose which fields are searchable in full mode (real database search)
custSearch.emptyShown: No fields selected — defaults will be used

custFields.name: Customer name
custFields.title: Customer fields
custFields.dragHint: Drag to reorder / toggle fields · core cannot be removed
custFields.optionsHint: Edit options of enabled select fields
custFields.noSelectField: No enabled select fields yet — enable some on the Customer fields page first

custForm.noTools: No tools enabled — enable them in CRM settings › Tools
custForm.toolSummaryNote: Summary will be pulled from the sales module next (via API)

workflow.title: Workflow
workflow.subtitle: The central engine that controls requests & approvals across modules
workflow.intro: Action requests usually arrive from other modules as a slip — or you can create them here. First we set up this central workflow engine; everything routes through it.

requests.d: The 'action request' forms people fill in (the slip that flows through).

numbering.d: Choose when the document number is issued — Create / Receive / Approve (per doc type).

steps.d: The lifecycle steps inside a document: draft → … → done.

routes.d: The document-type flow (CL→FO→QT→SO). Cut steps you don't use.

authorities.d: Who can create / do each stage / approve (with budget).

conditions.d: Branch the path by rules (amount, type, department…).

integrations.d: Let other modules call this engine and send slips in/out.

tracking.d: Track each request's status and full history.

workbox.d: Set which document boxes each role (MK/Telesale/Sale/Admin) sees

numbering.title: Document numbering
numbering.sub: Choose when the document number is issued (per document type)
numbering.note: One event per document type — the number is issued once when the chosen event happens.

eventHint.CREATE: Issue the number as soon as the slip is created
eventHint.RECEIVE: Issue the number when the receiver accepts the work

stages.title: Stages (document lifecycle)
stages.sub: Draft is always first, Done always last — add, remove or reorder the steps in between.
stages.head: first
stages.lockHint: Multiple people can work here — lock to avoid clashing edits.
stages.outcomeHint: Confirm the outcome at the end (e.g. sold / not sold).
stages.groupHint: Pick the data group this stage works on

routes.title: Route (document flow)
routes.sub: Order is fixed — you can only cut out removable steps. The active path updates below.
routes.none: This module is a single document — no onward route

auth.title: Authorities
auth.sub: Separate authority per capability — who can create, do each stage, and approve (with budget).
auth.uncovered: No authority covers this — no one can do it
auth.empty: No authorities yet — add one below.
auth.warnEmpty: No members selected — this won't resolve to anyone.
auth.levelNamePh: Level name, e.g. HR / HR head
auth.orderHint: If a user matches multiple frames, the top one (first) is used — reorderable
auth.noStages: No stages set yet — define them on the Stages page first

workbox.title: Work box per role
workbox.sub: Choose which document boxes each role sees (from the route's documents)
workbox.note: Each company can differ based on staffing/work split. Available documents come from the Route — cut a document and its box disappears.
workbox.noRoles: This module has no work roles defined

crmSettings.title: CRM settings
crmSettings.subtitle: Manage customer fields and access
crmSettings.enforceOnHint: On: only admins can open settings
crmSettings.enforceOffHint: Off: anyone can open settings (avoid locking yourself out)
crmSettings.willLoseAccess: Warning: you are not an admin; enabling will lock you out of this page
crmSettings.noAccess: You do not have access to these settings

custColumns.title: Table view (columns)
custColumns.dragHint: Drag to reorder · pick from special columns (code/status) and enabled fields only — show all or just some, your choice
custColumns.emptyShown: No columns selected — the table will use defaults

custStatus.title: Customer statuses
custStatus.hint: Enable the statuses you use and rename their labels · ACTIVE cannot be disabled and is the only status shown on the main page
custStatus.label: Customer statuses

comm.title: Communication log
comm.desc: Log messages / channels of contact with the customer

saleshistory.title: Sales history
saleshistory.desc: Summary of CL/FO/QT/SO for this customer (from sales)

custTools.title: CRM tools
custTools.hint: Choose which tools are enabled for customers · more tools coming

custHistory.confirmRevert: Revert the record to this version?
custHistory.noChange: No field changes in this version

custReq.title: Action request

list.clickToOpen: Click a row to open the request

flow.title: Forward document (FLOW)
flow.noFrame: No authority frame matches you — configure it in Authorities
flow.noReceiver: No receiver assigned for this stage yet
flow.noNext: No next stage (document will be completed)
flow.toGroupHint: Everyone in the group sees it in their inbox — first to accept takes the job
flow.toOneHint: Send to one specific person — only they see it in their inbox

custReq.uploadHint: Drag files here or click to choose
custReq.deleteHint: Delete draft (only at the draft stage, before sending)
custReq.deleteConfirmBody: Permanently delete request {{code}}? This cannot be undone.

recv.btnHint: Accept = record that you received it · Decline = bounce back to sender with a reason
recv.title: Receive document
recv.acceptHint: Record on the document that you have received it (so the sender knows), and move it to the 'In progress' box.
recv.declineHint: Send the document back to the sender with a reason (like a rejection).

custReq.sendHint: Review must fully pass before sending
custReq.ckSimMiss: Name {{pct}}% similar to existing — must be under 50%

salesFields.title: Sales document fields
salesFields.subject: Subject / Title
salesFields.groupDragHint: Drag fields into the group you want · drag to the right to remove

custReadiness.title: Readiness
custReadiness.subtitle: Define when a customer becomes ready to act on again
custReadiness.saleDesc: Ready again after the sale/service is done (some services take long — add a delay)
custReadiness.contactDesc: Ready again after no contact for at least the given period
custReadiness.calDesc: Ready when a calendar activity is near/overdue (not done)
custReadiness.saveErr: Failed to save to server — please try again

salesDoc.saveErr: Failed to save to server — please try again
salesDoc.confirmRecall: Recall document? (the other side hasn't received it)
salesDoc.sendWholeHint: Send to the group — first to receive gets it
salesDoc.dropFiles: Drag files here or click to choose
salesDoc.dupTitle: This customer is mid-sale — {{count}} open document(s)
salesDoc.noChain: No documents follow this set yet
salesDoc.name: Name
salesDoc.noCustSel: No customer selected — pick a customer first to view data and log tools/appointments
salesDoc.apptHintCl: Saved to the calendar (/inbox) · ref CL {{code}} · if not marked done by the due date, it returns to "To call"
salesDoc.noItems: No items yet — click "Add item" to pick a product/service or type
salesDoc.itemsCountHint: {{count}} item(s) · see totals below (click "Table" to edit)
salesDoc.selectLead: Select a name on the left to view data and log
salesDoc.openFoTitle: Open an FO referencing this CL
salesDoc.closeSettingsHint: Close strategies · lost reasons · attachment types · files required before close (QT)
salesDoc.addOption: Type and press Enter to add
salesDoc.cfgRequiredFiles: Files required before a deal can be won
salesDoc.cfgRequiredFilesHint: Pick attachment types that must be attached in customer tools before closing a won deal (e.g. payment slip) — none = not required
salesDoc.closeMissingFiles: Cannot close — missing required attachment(s): {{files}} (attach in Tools → Attachments first)
salesDoc.closeReqFilesNote: Required attachment(s): {{files}} in customer tools before closing
salesDoc.toQuoteNote: The fields above are pulled from the FO (Sale section) — after confirming, an "Open QT" button will appear to carry it forward
salesDoc.soCloseHint: Close when fully paid — marks the sale fully complete
salesDoc.soHandoffNote: Data prepared to hand off (production/project) — wire to SAP/Oracle/external systems later
salesDoc.soHandoffReady: Prepared for handoff — pending target-system integration
salesDoc.recvAcceptHint: Once received, the document enters your 'In progress' box and you become the owner
salesDoc.recvDeclineHint: Decline — the document is bounced back to the sender with a reason
salesDoc.ageNotStarted: Lifespan {{n}} days · starts when entering In-progress
salesDoc.closeCancelHint: Cancel this quotation (not a loss) — when several were opened and another won, cancel the rest
salesDoc.addQuoteHint: Create another quotation referencing the same opportunity (FO)
salesDoc.addQuoteConfirm: Create a new quotation referencing the same FO?
salesDoc.itemsLockedHint: Editable only in the draft stage
salesDoc.notHeldErr: This document isn't in your hands — can't save/close/delete
salesDoc.editReqMoreHint: Send this QT back to the creator's inbox to add more quotations (same FO) and resend
salesDoc.editReqRevisionHint: Send back to the creator to make a revision and resend
salesDoc.revisionConfirm: Make revision v{{n}}? (saves current as an old version · age resets to full, then edit)
